title: Greek Yogurt with Berries & Chia Seeds (High-Protein Breakfast) | Eati
introduction: Greek yogurt with berries and chia seeds is one of the fastest high-protein breakfasts you can make — 24g of protein and just 245 calories in under 5 minutes. Nonfat Greek yogurt provides slow-digesting casein protein, berries add fiber and antioxidants, and chia seeds turn the bowl into a satiety powerhouse. Track it in Eati to keep your breakfast macros consistent.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- 1 cup (245g) nonfat plain Greek yogurt
- 1/2 cup (75g) mixed berries (fresh or frozen)
- 1 tablespoon (10g) chia seeds
- 1 teaspoon honey or maple syrup (optional)
- Pinch of cinnamon (optional)
- 1 tablespoon chopped walnuts or almonds (optional)

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Spoon 1 cup of Greek yogurt into a bowl.
2. Top with mixed berries — thaw frozen ones for 30 seconds in the microwave if needed.
3. Sprinkle 1 tbsp chia seeds evenly across the top.
4. Drizzle honey and add a pinch of cinnamon if using.
5. Optional: add a tablespoon of chopped nuts for crunch and healthy fats.
6. Log your serving in Eati to keep protein and calories on target.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 245 kcal per serving (without honey/nuts)
Fat: 6g
Protein: 24g
Carbs: 22g (8g fiber, 13g natural sugars)

Tip: switch to 2% Greek yogurt for +40 cal and +1g fat but improved creaminess and satiety.

sections.heading: Why Greek Yogurt with Berries Works for Weight Loss
sections.content:
This bowl delivers an unusually strong protein-to-calorie ratio for a no-cook breakfast. Here's why it earns its place in almost every fat-loss plan:

Macro breakdown (estimated):
• 245 calories
• 24g protein (high)
• 6g fat
• 8g fiber
• ~10g protein per 100 calories — excellent

The casein advantage:
Greek yogurt is mostly casein protein, which digests slowly over 4–6 hours. That extended release suppresses hunger longer than whey or fast carbs, which makes it easier to space meals out on a calorie deficit. Studies in the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition show casein at breakfast reduces afternoon snacking by 200–300 calories.

Berries punch above their weight:
Blueberries, raspberries, and strawberries average 50–60 cal per cup while delivering 4–8g of fiber and polyphenols linked to lower inflammation. They're among the lowest glycemic fruits, so they pair beautifully with protein without spiking blood sugar.

Chia seeds multiply satiety:
One tablespoon of chia absorbs up to 10× its weight in liquid, which is why the bowl thickens if you wait 5 minutes before eating. Each tablespoon adds 60 cal, 3g protein, 5g fiber, and 3g omega-3 ALA — a big fiber payoff for a small calorie cost.

Compared to common alternatives:
• Flavored Greek yogurt cup (150g): 180 cal, 14g protein, 18g added sugar.
• Granola yogurt parfait: 420 cal, 12g protein, lots of added sugar.
• This recipe: 245 cal, 24g protein, 0g added sugar.

For more on strategic breakfast planning, see our [20 high-protein low-calorie breakfast ideas](/blog/20-high-protein-low-calorie-breakfast-ideas) and [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods). Use a calorie calculator to set your daily target.

sections.heading: How to Pick the Right Greek Yogurt
sections.content:
Not all Greek yogurt is equally useful for fat loss. Here's what to look for:

Look for these numbers (per 170g / 6 oz container):
• Nonfat plain: 100–110 cal, 17–18g protein, 6g carbs, 0g fat — best for aggressive fat loss.
• 2% plain: 130–140 cal, 17g protein, 6g carbs, 4g fat — creamy with moderate calories.
• Whole milk plain: 180 cal, 16g protein, 6g carbs, 9g fat — good satiety, best for maintenance.

Watch out for:
• Flavored yogurts: 15–20g of added sugar, lower protein.
• 'Greek-style' (not true Greek): half the protein of real Greek yogurt.
• Live cultures claim: every real Greek yogurt has them; ignore as a differentiator.

Best brands by price/protein:
• Fage Total 0%: 18g protein per 170g (industry leader).
• Chobani Plain Nonfat: 17g protein per 170g.
• Oikos Triple Zero: 15g protein per 170g, zero sugar.
• Store brand (Kirkland, Trader Joe's): Often identical macros at 40% off.

For plant-based eaters:
• Siggi's plant-based: 10g protein per 170g.
• Kite Hill Greek-style almond: 11g protein.
• Note: plant yogurts rarely match dairy Greek yogurt for protein density.

sections.heading: Variations and Serving Ideas
sections.content:
The base recipe is just a starting point. Here are 8 ways to vary it without wrecking the macros:

1. Tropical (330 cal, 23g protein):
• Swap berries for 1/2 cup diced mango + 1 tbsp toasted coconut flakes.

2. Chocolate-PB (340 cal, 28g protein):
• Stir in 1 tbsp natural peanut butter + 1 tsp cocoa powder + 1 tbsp chopped dark chocolate.

3. Apple-cinnamon (270 cal, 24g protein):
• Swap berries for 1 small diced apple + extra cinnamon + walnuts.

4. Savory (280 cal, 26g protein):
• Skip fruit/honey. Add cherry tomatoes, cucumber, za'atar, and olive oil.

5. Protein-boosted (340 cal, 44g protein):
• Stir in 1/2 scoop whey or plant protein powder. Blend for smoothness.

6. High-fiber (300 cal, 26g protein, 14g fiber):
• Add 2 tbsp ground flax + 1 tbsp hemp hearts + extra chia.

7. Dessert-style (310 cal, 24g protein):
• Add 2 crushed vanilla wafers + 1 tsp vanilla extract + berries.

8. Breakfast bowl (420 cal, 32g protein):
• Top with 1/4 cup granola + 1 tbsp nut butter + banana slices.

Serving variations:
• Mini parfait: layer yogurt, berries, and chia in a mason jar — great for grab-and-go.
• Smoothie bowl: blend yogurt + frozen berries for a thick spoonable texture.
• Frozen yogurt bark: spread the mix on parchment, freeze 2 hours, break into pieces.

For more dessert-style protein ideas, see [cottage cheese pizza bowl](/blog/cottage-cheese-pizza-bowl-recipe) — same concept, savory spin.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Storage & Time-Saving Tips
sections.content:
This bowl is one of the fastest to prep in bulk. Here's how to set up a week of breakfasts in 10 minutes:

Mason jar method (5 days in 10 minutes):
• Layer in 16oz mason jars, bottom to top: 1 tbsp chia, 1/2 cup berries, 1 cup yogurt. The chia sits in liquid overnight and thickens naturally by breakfast time.
• Keep in fridge up to 5 days.
• Stir before eating.

Freezer-friendly version:
• Mix yogurt + berries + chia in individual containers.
• Freeze 4 hours for frozen yogurt texture.
• Eat frozen as a healthy ice cream alternative (~230 cal).

Smart shortcuts:
• Buy frozen mixed berries in 5 lb bags — cheaper, same nutrition, no spoilage.
• Pre-portion chia seeds into weekly containers (1 tbsp each).
• Buy Greek yogurt in 32 oz tubs, not single cups (saves 40% on cost).

Storage rules:
• Assembled bowls: 5 days in fridge (jars) or 2 hours (open bowls).
• Unopened Greek yogurt: 2 weeks past 'best by' date usually fine.
• Chia seeds: 2 years in pantry, indefinitely in fridge.

Travel hacks:
• Airport-friendly: pack jar with lid + spoon in checked bag.
• Work breakfast: transport jar in insulated lunch bag with ice pack for up to 4 hours.

For more on tracking prepped meals, see [how to track calories with an app](/blog/how-to-track-calories-with-an-app). For the full breakfast cluster, see [20 high-protein low-calorie breakfast ideas](/blog/20-high-protein-low-calorie-breakfast-ideas).

midArticleCta: Hit your protein goal at breakfast in under 5 minutes. Log this Greek yogurt bowl in Eati and see exactly how it fits your daily plan.
conclusion: A 5-minute breakfast that delivers 24g of high-quality protein with just 245 calories. Make it your default high-protein breakfast — or meal prep 5 jars on Sunday — and track each serving in Eati to stay on plan.
metaTitle: Greek Yogurt with Berries & Chia Seeds: 24g Protein, 245 Calories (Easy Breakfast)
metaDescription: High-protein Greek yogurt bowl with berries and chia seeds — 24g protein in 245 calories. Ready in 5 minutes with meal prep tips, 8 variations, and yogurt-buying guide.
targetKeyword: greek yogurt berries chia seeds breakfast

faqs.question: How many calories in Greek yogurt with berries and chia seeds?
faqs.answer: About 245 calories per serving with 1 cup nonfat Greek yogurt, 1/2 cup mixed berries, and 1 tbsp chia seeds. Adds up to 24g of protein and 8g of fiber. Adding honey (+20 cal) or nuts (+50 cal) will raise the total.

faqs.question: Is Greek yogurt with berries a good breakfast for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Yes. It's one of the highest-ranked breakfasts for fat loss — 24g of casein protein keeps you full 4–6 hours, berries are low glycemic and high in fiber, and the whole bowl clocks in under 250 calories.

faqs.question: How much protein is in a Greek yogurt bowl with chia?
faqs.answer: About 24g per serving with nonfat Greek yogurt. Adding 1/2 scoop of whey protein boosts it to 36g+ for a post-workout breakfast. Chia seeds add 3g of plant-based protein per tablespoon.

faqs.question: Can I meal prep Greek yogurt bowls in advance?
faqs.answer: Yes — up to 5 days in mason jars. Layer chia, berries, and yogurt bottom-to-top. The chia thickens naturally overnight. Stir before eating. Avoid adding granola or nuts until serving to keep them crunchy.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Greek yogurt and regular yogurt for this recipe?
faqs.answer: Greek yogurt is strained, which doubles the protein (17g vs 8g per 170g) and halves the sugar. Regular yogurt gives you ~12g protein per cup; Greek gives ~24g. For fat loss, Greek yogurt is the clear winner.
